title: Solena nitida
intro: Solena nitida, also known as the hairy passionflower, is a perennial climber native to South America, characterized by its distinctive hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety and insomnia, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Solena nitida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is often used to treat restlessness, digestive issues, and skin conditions.

This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the Convolvulaceae family, Solena nitida is placed in the tribe Ipomoeae, which consists of various species of morning glories and related plants. The tribe Ipomoeae is further classified into the subtribe Ipomoeinae, which includes several genera of morning glories. The classification of Solena nitida is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
